summary: Barber Hub v3.9.4 — backend + frontend regression. 13/13 pytest pass, plus a separate MongoDB-level probe confirmed the vacation auto-reopen sweep flips is_on_vacation=false when vacation_until is in the past AND preserves vacation when vacation_until is in the future. Frontend: login loop FIXED (salon -> /dashboard, admin -> /admin), token/user/user_type persist in localStorage across refresh, two-column auth shell renders on desktop with data-testid='auth-hero-panel' at x=0 width=1001 height=900, true-black #000 body background on /auth AND /home (rgb(0,0,0)), shop-type-selector with male/female buttons visible and clickable on register form. register-barbershop with shop_type=female persists shop_type correctly (verified via 200 response body user.shop_type='female').

minor.issue: When the default tab 'With Email' is active, the input field has type='email'. Logging in with phone-only identifiers (admin, 09xxxxxxxx) triggers the browser-native '@ is missing' popup and BLOCKS POST /api/auth/login entirely. User must manually toggle to 'With Phone' tab. Consider making the input type='text' or auto-detecting phone vs email, or defaulting to 'With Phone' for admin-style accounts. Non-blocker but confusing.
Pydantic schema requires payment_method — documented in iteration_7 test fixtures update. Already enforced.

ui_bugs.issue: aria-pressed attribute is not set on [data-testid=shop-type-male]/[data-testid=shop-type-female] — selection is visually indicated (orange border) but screen-reader users cannot tell which is active. Minor a11y issue.

HTML5 email validation on type='email' field blocks login for phone-only accounts (admin, 0998765432). User must click 'With Phone' tab first. This was the original login-loop symptom's siblings — login() order fix is correct but UX can still trap users.

issues: Two-column layout renders correctly: left hero (x=0, w=1001) + right form. Body bg is pure #000000.
Body bg is rgb(0,0,0) — dark theme applied correctly.

backend: [DB-level probe] Sweep with past vacation_until -> modified_count=1, is_on_vacation flipped to False, vacation_until -> None
[DB-level probe] Sweep with future vacation_until -> modified_count=0 (correctly preserved)
frontend: AuthPage loads with data-testid='auth-page' and data-testid='auth-hero-panel' at {x:0,y:0,w:1001,h:900}
Salon login 0998765432/TestPass2026! -> POST /api/auth/login 200 -> redirect /dashboard -> LS has barber_hub_token(len=209) barber_hub_user_type='barbershop'
After reload on /dashboard URL stays /dashboard (persistence works)
Admin login admin/NewStrong2026!xYz (With Phone tab) -> POST /api/auth/login 200 -> redirect /admin
Register > Barbershop shows data-testid='shop-type-selector' with shop-type-male and shop-type-female buttons both visible and clickable

action_items: (MEDIUM UX) [data-testid=login-email-phone] uses type='email' on 'With Email' tab. Phone-only accounts (admin, 09xxxxxxxx) cannot submit without manually toggling to 'With Phone'. Recommend auto-detecting input or switching default tab for the admin phone.
(LOW A11Y) Add aria-pressed on shop-type-male/female buttons so screen readers reflect active selection.
(MINOR) /api/search/barbers param inconsistency from iteration_7 still open (accepts shop_type not type, missing approval_status filter).

(INFO) Master admin user.email is NOT 'mohamadalrejab@gmail.com' in DB — user was informed. The routeForEntity() branches on entity_type==admin anyway, so /admin redirect still fires. If the user wants the email-based master check to trigger, set db.admins.email='mohamadalrejab@gmail.com' for the admin record.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthPage.jsx: routeForEntity() uses user?.entity_type — but login() in App.js never merges the 'type' argument into userData; the entity_type comes from the user object returned by the backend (which DOES include entity_type for admin + shop_type for barbershop via the {k:v for k,v in entity.items() if k != 'password'} spread in server.py login handler). Worth confirming: backend does NOT inject 'entity_type' into the user dict before returning — it's only in the JWT. routeForEntity falls back correctly to shop_name-based detection, but the cleaner fix is to use the explicit user_type param from login() instead of user.entity_type. Works today by coincidence.

server.py _initial_vacation_sweep sleeps 3s before running but _vacation_autoreopen_task sleeps 15min BEFORE the first DB hit — meaning between the 3s sweep and the first 15min tick, any salon whose vacation_until lapses will stay 'on vacation' for up to 15 min. Consider doing the first iteration of the task immediately, then sleep. Low priority.
HTML5 email validation on login input is a recurring UX trap for phone-first users. Either use type='text' or write a JS-level validator that accepts both phone and email shapes.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical flows (login no-loop, role redirect, shop-type selector, dark theme, persistence)

admin.note: must use 'With Phone' tab — 'With Email' blocks via HTML5

test_credentials.salon_female_created_this_run: each test run creates a fresh TEST_female_<uuid8> salon via /api/auth/register-barbershop — cleanup NOT implemented

seed_data_creation: Two new pending barbershops created per run (TEST_male_<uuid8>, TEST_female_<uuid8>) by TestRegisterShopType. One TEST_v394_invalid_cycle subscription order attempt (400 rejected, not persisted). Salon 0998765432's vacation was toggled on (with past vacation_until) and then swept+cleaned by the direct-DB probe — final state: is_on_vacation=false, vacation_until=None.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Primary blocker (v3.9.4 login-loop) is FIXED — verified end-to-end. /app/backend/tests/test_v394_backend.py is the new regression suite (13 tests green). The vacation auto-reopen was verified at DB level (sweep query + update_many) because the in-process asyncio task has a 15-min cycle that tests cannot wait for. Pending follow-ups for main agent: (a) HTML5 email validation UX on login input, (b) aria-pressed on shop-type selector, (c) /api/search/barbers param name + approval_status filter from iteration_7.
rca of the issue: The original v3.9.4 bug — login loop caused by AuthPage.jsx passing (token, user) into App.js login() that expects (userData, accessToken, type) — is fully resolved. Current login() signature (line 86 of App.js) matches the call at AuthPage.jsx:144 and :170. Token now correctly stored in barber_hub_token localStorage key, user_type in barber_hub_user_type, and route redirect via routeForEntity() works: barbershop -> /dashboard, admin -> /admin. Reload preserves session.
